opportunities.title: Opportunities in July 2026
opportunities.description: Residencies, fellowships, grants, and open calls from the Paul & Daisy Soros Foundation, Ucross, AICA International, and more in our monthly list of opportunities for artists, writers, and art workers.

opportunities.title: 15 Books to Add to Your Summer Reading List
opportunities.description:
We’re highlighting books by NYFA-affiliated authors to bookmark for your summer reading–including a few that are hot off the presses! New York Foundation for the Arts’ flagship NYSCA/NYFA Artist Fellowship program offers grants in 15 artistic disciplines spanning literary, performing, and visual arts, and more. Below, we’re...

opportunities.title: Gwangju Biennale Announces Open Call for its Next Artistic Director
opportunities.description: In a marked break from the norm, the Gwangju Biennale Foundation—the organizer of Asia’s oldest contemporary art Biennale—has announced an open call seeking an artistic director for the 17th Gwangju Biennale, which is slated to take place in 2028. This is a fresh approach that the event is implementing in order to pay homage to […]

opportunities.title: The Box in Plymouth Wins UK’s Museum of the Year Award
opportunities.description: The Box in Plymouth, England, has been named the winner of this year’s UK Art Fund Museum of the Year Award. The annual £120,000 ($158,000) honor is the most prestigious museum prize in the nation and the largest of its kind in the world. The institution, located in the southwestern part of the country, beat […]

opportunities.title: How Craft Artists Are Creating Today
opportunities.description:
Representatives from five prestigious craft organizations fill us in on what they’re seeing. In this article, we’re checking in with residency programs and craft organizations nationwide to ask: What is the state of craft as a field today? In what new ways are craft artists creating using... 
 The post  How Craft Artists Are Creating Today  appeared first on  Nyfa .

opportunities.title: Celebrating the Cultural Community of Queens!
opportunities.description:
Get engaged with art of all disciplines in Queens through our City-funded grant programs! Queens Arts Fund, an annual City-funded program administered by New York Foundation for the Arts (NYFA), offers project grants to Queens-based artists, artist collectives, and small non-profit organizations of all artistic disciplines.
